meta.name: IDOR
meta.description: Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) test cases, bypass patterns, and methodology reference. Covers predictable IDs, horizontal/vertical privilege escalation, GUID bypass, and mass assignment.

payloads.description: Sequential ID probe — change your own ID (e.g., /users/1) to another user's ID
Order ID manipulation — access another user's order by guessing adjacent ID
IDOR combined with path traversal — some object references are used as file paths
User ID in query parameter — common pattern in older REST APIs
Vertical IDOR — access admin endpoint by supplying a lower-privilege user's ID
Impersonation endpoint — direct access to assume another user's session
Role escalation via direct object reference — modify another user's role field
UUID/GUID probing — replace your GUID with a predictable one or one found in other API responses
Predictable filename IDOR — admin/user-specific files with guessable naming patterns
JWT with userId:1 (admin) — alg:none JWT for user ID 1 (admin account)
Mass assignment role injection — extra field in request updates privileged properties
Mass assignment parameter pollution — include admin/balance fields not meant for user editing
Header-based IDOR bypass — override user context via custom headers accepted by some frameworks
Path normalization IDOR — /me/../2 may resolve to /users/2 after server-side normalization
